mentions.note: Leading housing indicator rallied 3%; owned in Charitable Trust. Could benefit if president forces institutional single-family home sales, boosting turnover.
Rallied again on housing recovery and consumer spending resilience; beneficiary of housing stock turnaround.
Up on Day 2; modest strength in housing-related retail.

Hit 52-week low, down 8%. 3% yield, good growth but ICE issues and lack of mortgage rate cuts holding back. Cramer regards as one of most problematic positions alongside AT&T. Waiting for mortgage rates to fall before adding.
Down 2.4%, new lows; Cramer's worst performer (along with Nike); massive negative signal on home/DIY spending.
Housing market in multi-decade low transaction volume; stock at 2-year low yesterday. Jumped 5% today on ceasefire; needs persistent rate cuts to sustain recovery.
Template rate-cut play; time-honored stock to own when Fed cuts; provides portfolio balance on tech-heavy days.
Eviscerated on rate expectations; needs housing turnover and potential Fed rate cuts to thrive; difficult near-term.
Been a huge bust due to higher interest rates and desktop decline; stock may rally if quarter isn't terrible but outlook weak.
Modest beat, 0.6% SSS below expectations, reiterated guidance; early May good; owned as hedge vs. rate cuts; needs housing recovery; if Fed cuts, regret selling.
3% yield is magical level. Can add to positions. Fed rate-hike scenario shouldn't derail DIY demand in spring selling season.
